item.title: Jiowana Sauto named in Force squad for Drua match
item.description: Fijian born front-rower Jiowana Sauto has been named in the Western Force squad for the 2026 Super W season and could face her former side, the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women, when the competition gets underway in Nadi this weekend. The 28-year-old brings a wealth of experience to the Western Force, having previously played for the Fiji Drua Women's, Melbourne Rebels and Queensland Reds.
item.content:encoded:
Fijian born front-rower Jiowana Sauto has been named in the Western Force squad for the 2026 Super W season and could face her former side, the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women, when the competition gets underway in Nadi this weekend.

The 28-year-old brings a wealth of experience to the Western Force, having previously played for the Fiji Drua Women's, Melbourne Rebels and Queensland Reds.

Sauto's ability to play at both prop and hooker provides valuable versatility for the side as they prepare for the new season.

The Force have also added Japanese international Yuna Sato and forward Keira Macaskill to their squad.

Sato returns for a second stint with the club after playing for the Force in 2023.

The second-rower has represented Japan 27 times and featured at last year's Women's Rugby World Cup.

Macaskill, a former Debby Hodgkinson Rising Star Award winner, returns after missing the 2025 season and can cover both the back row and front row after recently converting to prop.

The trio have been brought into the squad following injuries to Kahurangi Sturmey, Braxton Walker and Katalina Amosa.

The match presents an opportunity for Sauto to take on her former team in what is expected to be an exciting start to the season for both sides.

Western Force will begin their 2026 Super W campaign against the Fiji Drua at King Charles Park in Nadi at 2pm on Saturday after receiving a bye in the opening round.

item.title: First ever FIFA World Cup to feature 48 teams
item.description: First ever FIFA World Cup to feature 48 teams

item.title: Bitila Tawake to spearhead Fiji Drua women first home match
item.description: Bitila Tawake will captain and lead the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team during their first home match against the Western Force on Saturday. Karalaini Naisewa, Tawake and Tiana Robanakadavu will pack the scrum in the front rows, while Karavaki Lutumaibau and Carletta Yee will be at locks.
item.content:encoded:
Bitila Tawake will captain and lead the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team during their first home match against the Western Force on Saturday.

Karalaini Naisewa, Tawake and Tiana Robanakadavu will pack the scrum in the front rows, while Karavaki Lutumaibau and Carletta Yee will be at locks.

Nunia Daunimoala and Vice Captain Alfreda Fisher will flank the scrum as open and blindside flankers.

At half-back, Kolora Lomani will be leading the back three with Varanisese Qoro at fly-half, while Aqela Raitubu will be the outside winger.

Josivini Naihamu will play inside centre while Vika Nakacia will play outside centre.

Repeka Tove will play the blindside wing while Namere Rokowati at full-back.

The Drua Women have a formidable bench with Keleni Marawa and Zipporah Sorokacika, and Kelepi Lawavou will come the front rows, Vilisi Tivalele and Salaseini Railumu will cover the back rows, while Evivi Senikarivi, Litiana Vueti, and Michella'e Stolz will come the backs.

The Drua will face the Force this Saturday at the Churchill Park Lautoka at 2pm.

item.title: MGM U-18 Raluve Captain Rekisa wants to represent Drua and Fijiana in the future
item.description: 16-year-old Vasiti Rekisa has set her sights on representing the Fiji Women's Drua side and fulfilling her dream of one day wearing the Fijiana jersey on the international stage. Speaking to fijivillage News during the launch of the Weet-Bix Raluve Competition today, Rekisa says she is determined to continue developing her game and reach the highest level of women's rugby in Fiji.
item.content:encoded:
16-year-old Vasiti Rekisa has set her sights on representing the Fiji Women's Drua side and fulfilling her dream of one day wearing the Fijiana jersey on the international stage.

Speaking to fijivillage News during the launch of the Weet-Bix Raluve Competition today, Rekisa says she is determined to continue developing her game and reach the highest level of women's rugby in Fiji.

The team captain began playing in the competitive Raluve competition while in form three and has represented her side at the Under-18 level for the past two years.

Originally from Papua New Guinea, Rekisa says the experience gained through the Raluve competition has significantly boosted her confidence and sharpened her rugby skills.

She is looking forward to leading her team this season and remains confident they can deliver a strong performance in the competition.

MGM will play their first game this Saturday and the fixtures for the Southern Zone are yet to be released.

item.title: McKenzie set for 150th Super Rugby match in Chiefs semi-final
Hurricanes embrace favourites tag ahead of semi-finals
item.description: The Hurricanes are red-hot favourites to sweep past the Blues into a Shop N Save Super Rugby final but they insist there is "no ch

item.title: Ronaldo expected to feature as Portugal face Nigeria in friendly match
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo could get valuable game time tomorrow when Portugal take on Nigeria in their final warm-up match before the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Friday. The 41-year-old played the first half of Portugal's 2-1 victory over Chile last Sunday before being substituted at half-time, and is expected to continue his preparations for what is likely to be his final World Cup appearance.
item.content:encoded:
Cristiano Ronaldo could get valuable game time tomorrow when Portugal take on Nigeria in their final warm-up match before the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Friday.

The 41-year-old played the first half of Portugal's 2-1 victory over Chile last Sunday before being substituted at half-time, and is expected to continue his preparations for what is likely to be his final World Cup appearance.

Ronaldo has been named in Portugal's squad for the friendly and will be looking to sharpen his form ahead of the tournament in the United States.

The veteran striker heads into the World Cup after an extraordinary international career that has seen him score 143 goals in 227 appearances for Portugal and help his country win the UEFA European Championship in 2016.

Portugal, coached by Roberto Martinez, are among the favourites to win the World Cup and boast a talented squad featuring Bruno Fernandes, Bernardo Silva and Rafael Leao.

Nigeria, despite failing to qualify for the World Cup, will provide a stern test, having gone unbeaten in their last six matches.

Portugal will then fly to the United States and settle into their base camp in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida.

The FIFA World Cup will begin this Friday, and Portugal will open their campaign against the Democratic Republic of the Congo next Thursday at 5am before facing Uzbekistan on June 24th at 5am and Colombia on June 28th at 11.30am in their remaining Group K matches.

item.title: Drua Women set for home opener as Legge predicts strong performance
item.description: Ahead of their first home game of the season against the Western Force Women, McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's Coach Mike Legge says he is optimistic of a good result. When asked about last week's game against the NSW Waratahs, whether selecting 10 debutants was the best decision, Legge says they felt that last week's team was the best team that could get a result
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of their first home game of the season against the Western Force Women, McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's Coach Mike Legge says he is optimistic of a good result.

When asked about last week's game against the NSW Waratahs, whether selecting 10 debutants was the best decision, Legge says they felt that last week's team was the best team that could get a result

Legge highlighted this to the media, from the Drua base in Nadi saying that they had a long and honest review session yesterday, followed by two brutal session on the field.

He says that the team responded really well after the review session and it is one of the best sessions they have had so far.

At this point in time we are still awaiting confirmation on if Heat Protocols will be in place for this game.

Meanwhile, Karalaini Naisewa, Mereoni Nakesa, Josivini Naihamu, and Aqeula Raitubu were named in the team of the week.

The Fiji Drua Women will play their first home game against the Force 2pm Saturday at Churchill Park, Lautoka.

item.title: Eight arrested after disorder erupts at NBA Finals watch party in New York
item.description: Eight people were arrested and five police officers injured after disorder broke out during an NBA Finals watch party in New York

item.title: Bula Boys held to 2-all draw by Vanuatu in Friendly
item.description: The Bula Boys finished with a 2-2 draw against Vanuatu in their second FIFA International Friendly match this afternoon.Fiji opene
